name: Hokkaido
description: Japanese cuisine

name: Lou pasta
description: Italian cuisine

name: Lemoni Café
description: Small plates, also for take-away.

name: Chez Denise
description: Small, owner-operated bistro with traditional French country food in a nearly rustic setting. As such it's not exactly veggie-friendly.
price_range: Starters from €10-12, main courses €18-25, plus wine

name: À la Cloche des Halles
description: The bell no longer rings, but the brasserie-restaurant still has the flair from the old little bars from the good old times named "troquets".

name: La Robe et le Palais
description: Small restaurant serving mostly tasty Basque food. Fantastic choice of wines.

name: Café Marly
description: Part of theGrand Louvreredevelopment. Café Marly was opened in 1994 and is on the balcony on the northern terrace of the Cour Napoléon. Patrons can enjoy direct views of the Louvre Pyramid while sitting back in comfortable chairs, watching tourists stroll by.
address: 93, rue de Rivoli / cour Napoléon du Louvre

name: Macéo
description: What was once just a great wine bar with decent food has become a must-visit restaurant with the addition of star chef Thierry Bourbonnais. Second-empire atmosphere with fantastic food.
price_range: Starters €13-18 and main courses are €25-28. Vegetarian menu around €30.

name: Mystery Cuisine
description: The restaurant serves fusion cuisine inspired by French Vietnamese meals in an atmosphere perfect for couples.

name: Bar Hemingway
description: Hemingway tried to drink here once per week even before he made it big. In August 1944 Hemingway made a booze-powered drive into Parisaheadof the advancing Free French 2nd Tank Division to "liberate the Ritz", and specifically the bar which was shortly thereafter renamed in his honour. Today the bar is considered by many to be one of the best bars in the world, in no small part due to the bar-tending skills of Colin Field, who creates elaborate cocktails as a fine art, and with the rest of the staff is skilled at bringing his guests together in conversation.
price_range: Expect to pay €30 or more per drink

name: Café Oz
description: You probably didn't think you were coming to Paris to sample Australian culture, but if after a long day of strolling from one end of the city to another you would just like to let go a bit and meet up with some fellow anglophones then you could do a lot worse than this almost legendarily hard-partying Aussie joint (ask the neighbours). Warning: as with other Aussie places in Paris for some reason, weekend nights here tend to bring out hoards of young single Frenchmen looking to chat up some (any) visiting sheilas. This has been known to lead to, um, confrontations. ~€7 pints.

name: Juvénile's
description: The bistro serves many different wines from around the world and tapas. You can buy a bottle to take home if you like it.

name: Willi's Wine Bar
description: It's actually a restaurant and is more upscale than Juvéniles, serving good food and good-to-great bottles of wine with a focus on the Rhône Valley, but including many from Burgundy, the Loire, as well as Italians, and "Atlantic crossing" Californians. The dinner menu by chef François Yon Great won the "Bib Gourmet 2009" award, and there are cheeses & desserts (yummy crumble) for after. Reservations recommended.

name: Blend Hamburger
description: French meat with home made ketchup.

name: Le Bougainville
description: This comfy little bar offers home cooking from theAuvergneregion, and after-work drinks for the financial set.

name: Grillé
description: A fast food restaurant that makes a "healthy" version of kebab (good herbs, better salad). Three cooks, Marie Carcassonne, Hugo Desnoyer, and Frédéric Peneau worked on this creation.

name: Rani Mahal
description: This Indian restaurant is raved about by vegetarians. It serves meat as well.

name: Café Etienne Marcel
description: A Costes brothers creation but with lower prices than many of their other restaurants. The Etienne Marcel combines a creative menu with hip interior design and hip musical ambiance.Vegetarians:The crispy risotto balls (risotto croustillant) are amazing, and pretty much impossible to make at home.

name: Le Tambour
description: The really distinguishing feature of the Tambour is its very late hours.

name: Drouant
description: Anthony Clémot runs this French kitchen.

name: Café Noir
description: A particularly fine example of a Paris café.

name: La Cantine
description: A technology focused café and co-working space.

name: The Frog & Rosbif
description: As you probably know the English use the word "frog" as an ethnic slur for the French. You may not know that the word "rosbif" is a French ethnic slur for the English. Well, it seems that there are mostly no hard feelings over a pint, or so the presence of this chain of 6 English pubs in the French capital would tend to suggest.

name: [dead link]Harry's New York Bar
description: Legend has it that Harry's New York Bar was the birthplace of the Bloody Mary invented by Fernand "Pete" Petiot, an American bartender, and was another Hemingway hangout.

name: Camille
description: Fantastic fresh French food. It is worth the wait.

name: Chez Janou
description: Chez Janou has such a pretty location that it's worth an evening visit just for that. But the food and atmosphere are also amazing.  Vegetarians be warned, the tortellini isnotthe token veggie dish; there isn't one.

name: Page 35
description: The name apparently refers to some section of some newspaper or another apparently with social listings.  Thecartefeatures fantastic crêpe opportunities making this a must for lunch or lingering over acarafeor two of the house wine.

name: Le Petit Marché
description: This is one of those charming little Parisian bistros which is off on its own a little.  The wait staff couldn't possibly be friendlier so this is a good place to bring people to show them how nice Parisian dining can be.  The food is in a higher class than the price range, and the folks who work here have an easy collegial or even familial way with each other, and with frequent guests.

name: Le Potager du Marais
description: A 100% vegetarian restaurant specialized in re-creating the French country dining experience without any of the meat.

name: Andy Wahloo
description: Andy Wahloo apparently means "I don't have anything" in Arabic. The Moroccan artist who owns this place certainly means it as a play on words having done the decor as a Moorish take on Andy Warhol.

name: Les Arts et Métiers
description: An updated but otherwise standardBrasserie, the draw here is the crowd: young trendy locals love the place.

name: L'Attirail
description: Cool bar/club with concerts ranging fromJazz Manoucheto contemporary swing.

name: Café des Musées
description: A bog standard and absolutely unpretentious bistro, thedes Muséesis a great place to cool your heels over a glass or two of red or white after stomping around the Marais all day.

name: Café Solo
description: On a newly car-free street, the Solo has a huge music collection which they are in the process of selling off at €6 per CD.

name: Les Enfants Rouge
description: This one is makes for another tough call between theEatandDrinksections. It's a completely traditional French bistro with a popular bar in the evenings.

name: Léonard
description: If you like trendy décor this is your joint. Free Wi-Fi.

name: La Perle
description: A small corner café-bar well-known to the trendy people of Le Marais. Dozens of young and old people gather here every evening, especially on weekends. There are also few other cool bars to enjoy on the rue Vieille du Temple.
address: rue Vieille du Temple/rue de la Perle

name: The Quiet Man
description: This Irish pub has sessions and Irish music almost (but not quite) nightly. The atmosphere is tops for the genre.

name: La Villa Kéops
description: The motif is Egyptian, while the clientele appears to be almost entirely gay men. Free Wi-Fi.
